name: Joe Williams
abstract: Joe Williams (born Joseph Goreed; December 12, 1918 – March 29, 1999) was an American jazz singer. He sang with big bands, such as the Count Basie Orchestra and the Lionel Hampton Orchestra, and with small combos.

interviews.title: Joe Williams interviewed by Leslie Gourse, New York City, New York, March 9, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: John Altman
about_quotes.evidence: The transcript mentions Joe Williams only in the context of the interviewer's anecdote about overhearing a conversation; the interviewee (John Altman) does not mention Joe Williams or describe any personal interaction with him.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Altman interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, September 14, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Capp
about_quotes.evidence: What was it like working with Joe on that album? ... And that album that we did, “Live at the Century Plaza,” ... Joe just turned around and said to Nat, “B flat.” Nat put it down. And we played.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Capp interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, September 3, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Edison
about_quotes.evidence: they put us together on his first tour without the Basie band. And we stayed together for about three years. And about four years we travelled together.
about_quotes.doc_title: Harry Edison — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Goodwin
about_quotes.evidence: I got to work with him for a few weeks in the ‘60s and he wanted a trio with Jim Hughart and Mike Melvoin and I and we went up to Seattle and played
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Goodwin interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 28, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Butch Miles
about_quotes.evidence: Yeah, I worked off and on with Joe many times with the band... So I can in all honesty say Joe Williams was my best man when I got married.
about_quotes.doc_title: Butch Miles interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Scottsdale, Arizona, March 4, 1995 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Clark Terry
about_quotes.evidence: Well I’ll tell you Joe, I’ve had the pleasure of being a friend of yours and very close associate of yours for a very long period of time
about_quotes.doc_title: Clark Terry interviewed by Joe Williams and Michael Woods, Clinton, New York, May 19, 1995
